message:
refactor: migrate HashMap/HashSet to BTreeMap/BTreeSet for deterministic iteration across entire codebase

Replace all hash-based collections with B-tree-based collections throughout the
runtime, reactor, scheduler, plan, trace, transport, and channel subsystems.
This ensures deterministic iteration order everywhere, which is critical for
reproducible test/simulation results (especially the LabReactor chaos testing),
deterministic task scheduling and polling order in the executor, and consistent
logging and diagnostics output.

The tradeoff is a slight performance cost (O(log n) vs amortized O(1) for
lookups/insertions), which is negligible for the collection sizes involved in
reactor registrations, task tables, and obligation tracking.

Affected subsystems and changes:
- channel/partition: HashSet<(u64,u64)> → BTreeSet, adapt drain() to mem::take()
- lab/virtual_time_wheel: HashSet<u64> → BTreeSet for cancelled timer IDs
- obligation/leak_check: HashMap/HashSet → BTreeMap/BTreeSet, add Ord derives to ObligationVar
- plan/{certificate,mod,rewrite}: HashSet<PlanId> → BTreeSet, add Ord derives to PlanId
- runtime/deadline_monitor: HashMap → BTreeMap for monitored tasks + duration history, HashSet → BTreeSet
- runtime/{io_driver,local,obligation_table,task_table}: HashMap → BTreeMap for interests, local tasks, obligations, stored futures
- runtime/reactor/{io_uring,kqueue,lab,macos,windows}: HashMap → BTreeMap for registrations and sockets, update architecture docs
- trace/{certificate,filter}: HashSet → BTreeSet, add Ord derives to EventCategory
- transport/aggregator: HashMap/HashSet → BTreeMap/BTreeSet for PathSet, dedup state, reorderer; add Ord derives to PathId
- types/symbol: add PartialOrd/Ord derives to ObjectId and SymbolId (prerequisite for BTreeMap keys)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
